attacking_style: Does not initiate decisive moves early; instead times late surges perfectly on steep gradients (18-19%) in final meters, particularly on punchy uphill finishes. Excels at catching long-range efforts rather than launching them.
team_tactics: Operates with minimal leadout support, often without proper sprint train even when team commits resources. Functions as opportunistic finisher rather than protected sprint leader, relying on positioning over team control.
terrain_preference: Steep punchy climbs with gradients above 15%, especially short explosive finishes like Mur de Huy. Strong on hairpin sections where recovery capacity and uphill sprint speed create advantages. Struggles on sustained climbing efforts.
vulnerability: Tactical naivety when in winning position—shares workload unnecessarily allowing chasers to bridge. Passive racing when not the designated finisher, failing to counter-attack from favorable positions.
key_patterns: Wins by timing late surges on steepest sections, not early attacks (Romandie, Fleche Wallonne)
Superior recovery capacity allows bridging after appearing dropped on climbs (Poland 2018, uphill sprints)
Demonstrates tactical maturity sitting back early, then exploiting others' efforts in finale (Vuelta 2019, Romandie)
Strong uphill sprint speed nearly steals stages even from tactically superior positioned riders (Woods 2018)
Tactical errors when leading: shares pace allowing bridges rather than forcing others to chase (Paris-Nice 2020)
form_trajectory: positive: 2022 showed peak tactical maturity with two major wins (Fleche Wallonne, Romandie) using refined timing over raw power, winning without team support
